summary: Frontend-only route regression testing completed on https://wc2026-nine-sepia.vercel.app. Hash-route navigation is still functionally shifted/misaligned in deployed app (active tab changes, but main content shows previous/incorrect page). Requested route fix is not validated as fixed on live preview.

ui_bugs.issue: Route/component mismatch: #/schedule renders Home content while Schedule tab is active.
Route/component mismatch: #/groups renders Schedule content while Groups tab is active.
Route/component mismatch: #/bracket renders Groups content while Bracket tab is active.
Route/component mismatch: #/predictor renders Bracket content while Predictor tab is active.
Route/component mismatch: #/compare renders Predictor content while Compare tab is active.

integration_issues.issue: Could not validate route/query isolation because Compare/Predictor pages are not consistently mounted on their own routes due route-shift bug.
No-route-shift requirement fails: sequential direct navigation causes active nav to move correctly but displayed page content remains mismatched/lagged.

action_items: Validate deployed artifact/preview URL is serving the latest commit that removed route transition wrapper and updated route wiring.
Hard-verify service-worker/chunk invalidation on preview deployment (stale lazy chunks can map wrong components to routes).
After redeploy/cache-bust, re-run direct hash-route matrix: /#/schedule, /#/groups, /#/bracket, /#/predictor, /#/compare, /#/stats and confirm each page-specific marker.
Re-test Compare/Predictor query isolation only after route mapping is corrected.
critical_code_review_comments: Current source in workspace shows correct route declarations in App.jsx; observed failures indicate deployment/runtime artifact mismatch rather than obvious source mapping bug in reviewed files.
Nav hrefs are correctly hash-based (#/schedule etc.) and active tab state updates, but main route content does not align—this strongly suggests stale or wrong bundle/chunk behavior on live preview.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Used public preview URL https://wc2026-nine-sepia.vercel.app. Repro is deterministic: active nav matches requested hash route, but rendered main content corresponds to wrong/previous tab (schedule->home, groups->schedule, bracket->groups, predictor->bracket, compare->predictor). /#/stats probe also behaved inconsistently in sequence and ended on compare content in one run.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: open hash routes directly on preview in sequence and inspect both active nav and main content text. Active link changes correctly but displayed page markers are shifted/mismatched. Probable RCA is deployment/runtime artifact mismatch (stale lazy chunks/service worker cache invalidation issue) rather than current workspace route-source mapping, because App.jsx route declarations appear correct. Mitigation: ensure latest commit is actually deployed at tested URL, invalidate SW/caches/chunks for preview, then re-run hash-route matrix and compare/predictor isolation checks. MOCKED APIs: NONE.
